description_long: This bungalow features a sprawling, low-profile structure with a complex, multi-faceted brown hip roof that extends over several protruding wings. The exterior walls are a mix of yellow and black, with the yellow sections featuring prominent window frames and awnings, while the black sections are more solid and less fenestrated. Multiple window groupings are visible, some arranged in clusters and others spaced apart, contributing to an asymmetrical facade. The roof has several overhangs and varying elevations, giving the building a dynamic silhouette. There is no visible porch, but the entryways and windows suggest a residential function. The overall design is modern with hints of traditional bungalow proportions and massing.
description: A modern bungalow with a complex brown hip roof, yellow and black exterior walls, and asymmetrical window groupings. The structure is low and wide, with multiple roof elevations and no visible porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main yellow facade with grouped windows and a central black section, highlighting the wide hip roof.
description_view_1: Angled view revealing the transition between yellow and black wall sections, with more visible window groupings and a prominent roof overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the expansive brown roof and mostly black walls, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_3: Side view showing the intersection of yellow and black walls, additional window clusters, and the layered roof structure.
